subject: feat(session-undone): address 5 items from session-end undone pass
body:
Session trigger `undone` surfaced 5 items — each properly fixed rather
than skipped or deferred. The big one is a real new user-facing
feature (Check for Updates in Settings); the rest are adoptions,
corrections, and doc sync.

New user feature — Check for Updates (web only):
- SettingsModal: new "Check for Updates" menu item with cloud-download
  icon, loading spinner during the check, accessibility labels. Only
  renders when onCheckForUpdates is provided (the parent decides
  based on Platform.OS).
- home.tsx: wires `usePWAUpdate().checkForUpdates()` to the `useToast()`
  context. Each discriminator of CheckForUpdatesResult maps to an
  appropriate toast: 'no-sw' / 'done'+up-to-date / 'error-sw' /
  'error-version' / 'error'. The 'done'+hasUpdate branch is silent
  because the Update button in the home header already signals.
- This is the first real consumer of the typed CheckForUpdatesResult
  discriminator; previously the return was fire-and-forget.

HTTPS_PROXY pattern — adopted (adapted):
- scripts/check-player-ids.ts now installs an undici ProxyAgent as
  the global dispatcher when HTTPS_PROXY / HTTP_PROXY env vars are
  set, so Supabase's internal fetch calls route through the proxy
  transparently. Graceful fallback if env var unset OR undici can't
  be imported. Adapted from the glow-props reference (which
  hand-rolls https.get() with HTTP CONNECT) because Supabase owns its
  own fetch and can't be routed via the stdlib path.
- CLAUDE.md § Pattern Adoption Status: HTTPS_PROXY flipped from
  "Applicable but unimplemented" to "Adopted (adapted)".

TIMER_LEAKS triage — corrected:
- Discovered via `curl` against glow-props GitHub Pages that
  TIMER_LEAKS.md was ALREADY published upstream; the local triage
  record's "Action required: open upstream PR" was stale.
- Updated docs/TODO.md § Pattern Triage Record to reflect completed
  status.
- CLAUDE.md § Code Standards → Timer and Subscription Cleanup: fixed
  the broken `docs/implementations/TIMER_LEAKS.md` local reference
  (folder was deleted in a prior session) to point at the published
  https://devmade-ai.github.io/glow-props/patterns/TIMER_LEAKS.md URL.

TODO.md live-browser entry — reorganized:
- Split into "already covered by `npm run verify:inline-scripts`"
  (lists the specific headless-verified assertions) vs "requires
  real browser session" (true manual QA). Prevents verifiers from
  redoing headless-proven scenarios. Also added an item for the new
  SettingsModal → Check for Updates button so it gets manually
  exercised once.

Z.sheet tier — semantics clarified:
- constants/zIndex.ts: documented that Z.sheet (30) is for NON-modal
  persistent sheets/drawers — sits below Z.backdrop (40) by design
  so a modal opening over a sheet correctly covers it. Modal bottom
  sheets like SettingsModal continue to use Z.backdrop + Z.modal.
- CLAUDE.md § Z-Index Scale row updated to match.
- No current consumer, kept for glow-props fleet parity.

Verification:
- npx tsc --noEmit: 0 errors
- npm run verify:inline-scripts: 47 passed, 0 failed
- EXPO_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY="" npx tsx scripts/check-player-ids.ts:
  exits cleanly on missing key (basic syntax + module-transpile check)
- HTTPS_PROXY=... EXPO_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY="" npx tsx <script>:
  logs the "[proxy] Routing HTTPS traffic via ..." message, confirming
  the proxy-install code path runs before createClient

Tags: pwa, notifications, types, proxy, docs, fleet-alignment
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing, internal
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: fleet-alignment
Semver: minor
